steps.title: Identify the clause types
steps.content: The left side—”While one requires oxygen and one does not”—is a dependent clause (starts with “While” and cannot stand alone). The right side—”aerobic and anaerobic respiration are both forms of cellular respiration”—is an independent clause.

steps.title: Apply the introductory clause punctuation rule
steps.content:
A comma must follow an introductory dependent clause. Choice C — not, aerobic correctly places a comma after “not.”

The others violate the rule:
  • A provides no punctuation
  • B uses a period (creating a fragment from a dependent clause)
  • D misuses a semicolon (only for independent clauses)
.

C is correct.
